title: ARC-HACK-001 [P0] Implement Circle payment intent and verification flow for priced agent actions
objective: Create the first working economic action flow so a buyer can request a paid artifact, satisfy payment requirements, and unlock execution.
approvedScope: define a billable action catalog for at least three artifact types
add a payment intent or payment requirement endpoint for the buyer flow
add a payment verification path that marks the job as economically unlocked
keep the implementation testnet or mock-safe only
acceptanceCriteria: the system can present a price for at least one artifact
the API can verify a completed payment or test payment signal
the job lifecycle advances only after valid payment confirmation
externalAccessGaps: production-grade credentials can be stubbed if missing as long as interfaces are real and demo-safe
executionRules: optimize for a working demo path rather than a broad payment matrix
keep every payment flow auditable and reversible in test mode

title: ARC-HACK-002 [P0] Add Arc-side job, proof, and agent-economy adapter for Nexus work units
objective: Represent Nexus work as an economic unit that can be mapped to Arc-side jobs, proofs, or agent identity/reputation flows.
approvedScope: define the minimal Arc integration surface needed for the demo
map a Nexus task lifecycle to an Arc economic action or job representation
persist proof metadata that can be shown in the UI as economic evidence
acceptanceCriteria: there is a concrete Arc adapter layer in the codebase
a paid job can be associated with Arc-facing metadata or proof
the demo can explain what is stored or referenced on the Arc side
externalAccessGaps: if full Arc onchain write access is missing, keep the adapter real and the write path safely stubbed
executionRules: build the thinnest credible Arc integration that still demonstrates economic coordination

title: ARC-HACK-003 [P0] Bridge paid jobs into Nexus orchestration, review, and delivery unlock
objective: Make payment, execution, review, and delivery behave like one coherent lifecycle instead of disconnected demo steps.
approvedScope: create a paid-job state model
open or reserve Nexus work only after payment is unlocked
block delivery until review or validation is complete
persist timestamps and status transitions for the audit trail
acceptanceCriteria: a paid request creates a visible Nexus-side lifecycle
review becomes a first-class gate before delivery
the UI can show the full state progression from request to unlock
executionRules: prefer an explicit lifecycle state machine over hidden flags

title: ARC-HACK-004 [P0] Build buyer UI for request, payment status, job status, and artifact delivery
objective: Turn the current research dashboard into a buyer-facing economic flow that judges can understand in under 90 seconds.
approvedScope: add artifact selection and pricing presentation
show payment state, job state, review state, and delivery state clearly
preserve the premium operational feel of the current dashboard direction
support desktop-first demo with acceptable mobile fallback
acceptanceCriteria: the UI can demonstrate the economic flow end to end
the user can distinguish pre-payment, in-progress, in-review, and unlocked states
the vertical demo still clearly communicates paper-only safety
externalAccessGaps: wallet polish can be stubbed as long as payment state transitions are visible
executionRules: optimize the screen for live demo readability rather than feature breadth

title: ARC-HACK-005 [P1] Reuse ToTheMoonTokens as a paid research-artifact vertical in strict paper mode
objective: Expose at least one strong vertical workload that proves the agentic-economy model with real product value.
approvedScope: package backtest, walk-forward, or risk-audit outputs as purchasable artifacts
reuse existing research endpoints wherever possible
keep all outputs explicitly paper-only and research-only
acceptanceCriteria: at least one real artifact type can be bought and delivered
the final report is understandable to a judge without product context
nothing in the vertical implies live-trading readiness
executionRules: keep the vertical valuable but safe
prefer one polished artifact over many shallow ones

title: ARC-HACK-006 [P1] Add audit trail, observability, and end-to-end demo evidence for judges
objective: Make the demo credible by showing proof of status transitions, payment verification, review gating, and final delivery.
approvedScope: capture structured events for request, payment, execution, review, and delivery
add at least one end-to-end test or scripted validation path
prepare evidence artifacts for local demo and submission material
acceptanceCriteria: the team can replay the main demo path reliably
there is enough logging or evidence to explain what happened at each step
the system fails clearly when a required step is missing
executionRules: judge confidence comes from visible evidence, not hidden claims

title: ARC-HACK-007 [P1] Finalize submission assets, architecture summary, screenshots, and demo script
objective: Convert the working build into a clear submission package that judges can understand quickly.
approvedScope: finalize title, short description, and long description
prepare architecture summary and screenshot set
write the live demo script and submission checklist
prepare Circle product feedback notes
acceptanceCriteria: submission copy is ready to paste into the form
the team has a clear 90-second and 3-minute demo path
screenshots and architecture narrative match the shipped build
externalAccessGaps: video recording can happen at the end
executionRules: keep the story tight: paid agent work, validated delivery, safe vertical demo

title: ARC-HACK-008 [P0] Wire pitch site to live backend with graceful mock fallback
objective: Wire the pitch site to consolidated hackathon endpoints without breaking the live demo when the backend is offline.
approvedScope: replace static mock data with fetches from /api/market/signals, /api/agents/:id, /api/payments/:intent_id/flow
keep graceful fallback to local mock data when the backend is unreachable or the endpoints are not merged yet
surface loading, empty, and degraded states clearly in the pitch flow
acceptanceCriteria: pitch site displays live backend data when the consolidated endpoints are available
pitch site falls back to mock data without breaking the demo when the backend is offline
the UI makes it clear when the view is using fallback data instead of live backend state
externalAccessGaps: wait until the consolidated endpoints from feature/hackathon-arc-agent-market are merged into main or equivalent ARC-HACK backend work lands
outOfScope: removing the mock layer entirely before the backend is stable
executionRules: prefer a thin adapter layer over scattering fetch logic across every component
ensure robust error handling and fast fallback for live demo reliability
